Are black holes not just vacuum cleaners, but the archives of the universe?
threads: Чёрные дыры оказались не просто бездонными ямами, а настоящими «библиотеками» — информация, которую они поглощают, меняет их вибрации. Параметр «бремени памяти» позволяет прочесть прошлое дыры по гравитационным волнам. Это связывает квантовый мир с космическими масштабами.

title: Record Gamma-Ray Burst: 7 Hours of Invisible Light
oneliner: Astronomers captured a record gamma-ray burst: it lasted nearly 7 hours — hundreds of times longer than usual.
description: GRB 250702B broke all the rules: its 7-hour duration didn't fit old models. The solution? A merger of a black hole with a helium star. This event links ultra-long gamma-ray bursts with supernovae and gravitational waves.
abstract: Astronomers have found the longest cosmic explosion — a gamma-ray flash lasting nearly 7 hours. Usually, such bursts last seconds. This record-breaker seems to be caused by a black hole falling into a star that then explodes from the inside — like a soap bubble popping under pressure from its center. What else do the interiors of giant stars conceal?
threads: Гамма-всплеск длиной в 7 часов — как поезд, который не кончается. Такие рекорды ломают старые модели и указывают на новый сценарий: чёрная дыра, падающая внутрь гелиевой звезды и взрывающая её изнутри. Возможно, это ключ к пониманию самых мощных взрывов и источников гравитационных волн.

title: Quantum Dance at Room Temperature
oneliner: Physicists have synchronized the spins of two particles in a flat material at room temperature for the first time.
description: In a foil-like layer of boron nitride, an electron and a carbon atom's nucleus became partners in a quantum dance. Their bond held at room temperature—previously only possible in ultra-cold setups. This breakthrough promises compact quantum devices and ultra-sensitive sensors.
abstract: Imagine two spinning tops that stay perfectly in sync, even when separated—that's how entangled quantum particles behave. Physicists have now achieved this between electron and nuclear spins in a 2D material at room temperature, and used it to improve magnetic sensors. What else could we measure with this 'magical' link?
threads: В тонкой плёнке, как из кухонной фольги, физики заставили две частицы синхронно танцевать, даже когда те находятся далеко друг от друга. Такая магия происходит при комнатной температуре. Это ключ к сверхчувствительным датчикам, которые смогут различать отдельные молекулы.

title: Mysterious Red Dots: Light from a Gravitational Trap
oneliner: A new hypothesis casts these enigmatic red objects as 'counterparts' of black holes without an event horizon: at their core, matter shifts into a phase with negative energy.
description: Astronomers have spotted strange red dots in the early Universe. They might be objects resembling black holes but lacking an event horizon. When a star collapses, its matter undergoes a phase change into a negative-energy state, releasing hidden heat. Monstrous gravity saps light of its energy, redd
abstract: Perhaps the mysterious 'little red dots' are not distant galaxies, but objects born from matter transitioning into a state of negative energy. The energy is released deep inside, where gravity is so strong that light reddens to the extreme. Imagine a light bulb shining from the bottom of a bottomless well — we see only a dim, reddish glimmer.
threads: Странные красные точки в древней Вселенной могут быть «двойниками» чёрных дыр без горизонта событий. Их свет краснеет от гравитации, а питает их скрытая энергия фазового перехода вещества в состояние с отрицательной массой.

title: Black Hole Tugboat: Birth of a Stellar Core
oneliner: Supermassive black holes can not only destroy but also build dense stellar cores at the centers of galaxies.
description: It was thought black holes kick stars out of galactic centers, yet a third of giant galaxies host dense clusters snug against the hole. The clue? The hole's post-merger motion: like a cosmic tugboat, it can tow a whole swarm along.
abstract: Scientists have found a new way that huge galaxies can form star clusters in their centers, even right next to a giant black hole. Previously, it was thought they couldn't coexist: the black hole scatters stars. But it turns out, after galaxies and their black holes collide, a 'kicked' black hole drags some stars along with it like a tugboat, forming a bright core. Could such a cluster harbor secrets of its galaxy's past?
threads: Чёрная дыра после слияния галактик получает удар, как от гравитационной пушки, и начинает двигаться. Если скорость в самый раз, она не теряет, а собирает вокруг себя рой звёзд — и в центре галактики загорается плотное ядро, которое можно увидеть в телескоп.

title: AI Bridge Between Cosmos and Particles
oneliner: A new AI tool unites three disparate fields of physics to jointly search for nature's hidden laws.
description: ArgoLOOM is an AI-based program that builds a bridge between calculations from cosmology, particle physics, and nuclear science. It combines different computational methods into a single platform, enabling scientists to construct a unified picture of fundamental forces. Early tests suggest that this
abstract: An AI agent named ArgoLOOM works like a translator between different branches of physics — cosmology, collider physics, and nuclear science. It links their computational tools to search for new fundamental interactions. Scientists have already tested it on simple tasks. What if this very assistant finds the key to new physics?
threads: Раньше астрономы, физики с коллайдеров и ядерщики работали в разных песочницах. Новый AI-алгоритм ArgoLOOM соединил их игры в одну — и теперь мы можем найти то, что упускали поодиночке.

title: Light with One-Way Motion: The Magic of Rydberg Atoms
oneliner: Physicists created conditions where light only moves forward, generating stable rhythmic oscillations.
description: Scientists used special Rydberg atoms at room temperature to make light move in only one direction. This motion triggers collective oscillations, similar to a time crystal. The discovery helps understand how to control light in tiny optical chips and non-equilibrium systems.
abstract: Light usually travels both ways, but many devices need a one-way valve. Scientists used atoms in a special state (Rydberg) that, by synchronizing, force light to go only in one direction, as if creating a time crystal—a system that 'ticks' without external push. Why does the thermal motion of atoms help rather than hinder this effect?
threads: Свет может идти из точки А в точку Б, но обратно не возвращается — как поезд по кольцевой. Такой «односторонний» свет недавно заставили колебаться подобно вечному двигателю.

title: Space Beacons: LST-1 Telescope Begins Tracking
oneliner: The prototype LST-1 telescope has successfully tracked the pulsation of gamma rays from distant space beacons — blazars.
description: For two years, the LST-1 prototype tracked the flickering of distant space beacons — blazars. At their centers, supermassive black holes act like giant spotlights, shooting out narrow beams of gamma rays. The telescope didn't catch the rays themselves, but their atmospheric footprints — faint blue f
abstract: Scientists tested the future largest ground-based gamma-ray telescope on bright cosmic objects—blazars, which are like lighthouses of the Universe. It turned out the telescope can see perfectly well how their radiation changes over time. It’s like monitoring the pulse of distant galaxies. What else are these cosmic messengers hiding?
threads: Маяки Вселенной — блазары — мерцают гамма-лучами. Питает их сверхмассивная чёрная дыра. Телескоп LST-1 впервые детально проследил, как меняется этот пульс. Такие наблюдения приближают разгадку главного ускорителя природы.

title: Failed Star Shakes Up a Phosphine Cocktail
oneliner: The James Webb Space Telescope has reliably detected phosphine for the first time in the atmosphere of a cool brown dwarf — and it turned out to be ten times more than predicted.
description: Using Webb, astronomers peered into the atmosphere of the brown dwarf Wolf 1130C and found phosphine — a gas familiar from Jupiter. Its abundance is ten times higher than expected, pointing to vigorous mixing of layers, like in a planetary mixer. Now the boundary between giant planets and brown dwar
abstract: In the atmospheres of brown dwarfs (failed stars) and giant planets, phosphine was expected, but until now it was barely seen. Using the JWST telescope, scientists finally found a lot of phosphine in the dwarf Wolf 1130C: roughly like a spoonful of salt in a swimming pool. This discovery shows that the chemistry there is more complex and more like Jupiter's. Are such surprises hiding in exoplanets too?
threads: Коричневый карлик Wolf 1130C, «неудавшаяся звезда», удивил учёных: в его атмосфере нашли в десять раз больше фосфина, чем ждали. Этот газ, знакомый по Юпитеру, намекает на бурное перемешивание слоёв. Теперь астрономы смогут точнее отличать планеты от звёзд-неудачников.

title: Black Holes: A Rip Instead of an Event Horizon
oneliner: Math hints: black holes aren't gateways to other worlds, but dead ends where the fabric of space itself rips.
description: Combining the expansion of the universe with a black hole, physicists have found that at the event horizon, spacetime doesn't just bend — it tears, with curvature shooting to infinity. Inside, the ban on faster-than-light energy flow is violated, making the hole unstable. This challenges the usual i
abstract: The universe expands, and black holes are considered isolated. But the exact solution to Einstein's equations for a hole in an expanding cosmos has shown: at radius 2M, spacetime bursts like a popping soap bubble. This means familiar black holes might be an illusion. Could it be that nature forbids their existence?
threads: Обычно чёрная дыра — это область, откуда не вырваться. Но свежий анализ точного решения Эйнштейна показал: на месте горизонта событий может скрываться разрыв пространства-времени. Если это так, привычные чёрные дыры в нашей Вселенной могут оказаться невозможными.

title: The Invisible Planet in a Carbon Cocoon: How a Hot Neptune Cheated Death
oneliner: The James Webb Space Telescope peered into a scorching planet's atmosphere and spotted a carbon cocoon saving it from death.
description: LTT-9779 b is a planet from the 'hot Neptune desert', a region where worlds usually perish. But it survived thanks to a carbon cocoon: an atmosphere of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide turned out to be so thick that it doesn't evaporate even at 2000°C. On top of that, this exoplanet reflects 80% o
abstract: Planet LTT-9779 b is a hot Neptune, orbiting its star in just 19 hours. Its atmosphere, as revealed by the Webb telescope, is rich in carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide and is 500 times heavier than solar. This dense atmosphere evaporates more slowly, like a thick soup, allowing the planet to survive in the hot Neptune desert. What secrets do these rare worlds hide?
threads: Планета там, где её быть не должно: в «пустыне горячих нептунов» астрономы нашли мир с атмосферой из угарного и углекислого газа. Тяжелые молекулы, словно густой кисель, не дают ей испариться под лучами звезды.

title: Quantum Triplet: Three-Photon Entanglement Confirmed
oneliner: For the first time, physicists have convincingly shown that three photons from a special source form a single entangled entity.
description: A cooled resonator experiment has conclusively shown three-photon entanglement, specifically of the 'non-Gaussian' kind. A strict mathematical test excluded chance by a wide margin. This triple connection promises more robust quantum computing.
abstract: For the first time, physicists have reliably witnessed that light particles (photons) can be born in triplets and truly entangled by an invisible bond — like three voices singing in unison from any distance. This discovery promises a new resource for quantum computing and secure communication. And what if we change the 'rhythm' at which we detect them?
threads: Три фотона, рождённые в сверхпроводящем устройстве, оказались единым целым — их квантовое состояние нарушило все классические границы. Это первый достоверный случай тройной запутанности без лишних гипотез. Шаг к квантовым компьютерам, где три кубита будут крепче двоих.

title: How to Watch the Electron Dance in Living Cells
oneliner: Laser pulses have made it possible to 'film' the invisible magnetic dance inside living cells for the first time.
description: Biological processes depend on magnetic fields inside cells. A new method uses paired laser flashes to see invisible chemical 'dances' in living systems—a step toward portable sensors.
abstract: Inside our cells, reactions take place with radical pairs—molecules sensitive to magnetic fields, like tiny compasses. Previously, they couldn't be seen because they don't glow. Scientists came up with two new microscopy methods using light pulses that made these reactions 'light up.' This opens a window into the quantum world of the living cell. What other secrets does our body hide?
threads: Электроны в клетках ведут себя как парные магнитики. Их танец меняется от слабого магнитного поля, но увидеть его мешала невидимость самих частиц. Учёные применили лазерные вспышки — как корм для невидимых рыбок, — чтобы подсветить реакцию. Теперь можно заснять этот квантовый танец напрямую.

title: Cells Communicate with Light
oneliner: The cells' faint glow turned out to be the key to their state.
description: By comparing the glow of healthy and cancerous brain cells, scientists discovered different flickering patterns. This is a step toward diagnosis without surgery.
threads: Ваши клетки общаются светом, который в миллиард раз слабее солнечного. У здоровых и раковых клеток этот свет мерцает по-разному. Учёные считают, что в будущем по такому мерцанию можно будет выявлять болезни без биопсии — быстро и без боли.

title: Cloud Spices: How a Neural Network Improves Climate
oneliner: A hybrid AI method is 4–10 times more accurate at predicting clouds' impact on planetary heating.
description: Climate models err due to small clouds. Scientists trained a neural network on detailed calculations and transferred its 'experience' to the global scale. Cloud-free sky physics is computed classically — the method works at any level of greenhouse gases.
abstract: Tiny clouds, invisible to global climate models, powerfully influence how the atmosphere heats up. A new machine learning approach 'peeks' at their effect in ultra-high-resolution simulations where clouds are clearly visible. Errors shrank by 4–10 times—as if a blurry snapshot turned crystal clear thanks to a clever algorithm. Could this help us predict climate more accurately?
threads: Обычные климатические модели плохо учитывают небольшие облака. Исследователи обучили искусственный интеллект на сверхдетальных симуляциях, чтобы он точно предсказывал, как облака нагревают или охлаждают воздух. Гибрид из физических законов и нейросети уменьшил ошибки в 4–10 раз и открыл путь к более надёжным климатическим прогнозам.

title: Sky Hotspots: The Senders of Ultra-Particles Identified
oneliner: Neutron star mergers in nearby galaxies explain the clumps of cosmic rays in the sky.
description: Record-breaking energy particles point to nearby galaxies where rare neutron star collisions occur. This explains the mysterious hotspots and differences in observations by various telescopes.
abstract: Cosmic rays are particles with enormous energy, flying in from the depths of the universe. Scientists have discovered that their sources are likely in nearby galaxies, where neutron stars collide. It's like tracing footsteps back to specific houses on our cosmic street. Could our stellar neighbors really be the accelerators?
threads: Частицы самых высоких энергий, летящие к Земле, словно помечены адресами ближайших галактик. Семь из десяти ярчайших соседей совпадают с горячими точками на небе — случайностью это не объяснить. Возможно, их источник — столкновения нейтронных звёзд.

title: The Rings of Chariklo: A Dance of Dust and Light
oneliner: In Webb's infrared light, Chariklo's rings revealed a strange dance: the inner one gained density, the outer nearly vanished.
description: A cosmic dance of dust and light: the James Webb Space Telescope discovered that Chariklo's inner ring suddenly grew denser, while the outer one almost disappeared. Such adornments are likely short-lived and constantly renew themselves.
abstract: The James Webb Space Telescope peered through the rings of the asteroid Chariklo as it passed in front of a star. The inner ring became denser, as if a veil thickened, and the outer ring became almost transparent in infrared light, as if fog dissipated. So, rings of small bodies can change faster than we thought. Could a ring disappear right before our eyes?
threads: Космический телескоп «Уэбб» разглядел странности в кольцах астероида Харикло: внутреннее стало плотнее, а внешнее почти исчезло. Возможно, такие кольца — не вечное украшение, а временные структуры, которые постоянно обновляются и разрушаются.

title: Quantum Battery Powered by Thermal Chaos
oneliner: A warm environment can charge a quantum battery all by itself — no outlets or wires needed.
description: Scientists have created a quantum battery that charges from heat alone. Many particles, like an orchestra without a conductor, exchange energy in unison through a shared medium. The device is immune to interference and could simplify the operation of quantum computers.
abstract: Scientists have invented a quantum battery that charges itself just by being in a warm place, like a sponge soaking up water. No wires or controls needed—energy comes straight from the environment. Picture this: the heat all around us could one day power future computers! How might that reshape technology?
threads: Тепло — это хаос. Но квантовые частицы могут превратить его в электричество, просто «договорившись» через общую среду. Новая батарея без проводов сама черпает энергию из тёплого окружения и даже способна ускорить квантовые компьютеры.

title: Artificial Intelligence Unravels the Mysteries of Cosmic Radio Bursts
oneliner: AI detected nearly 6,000 bursts from a single object, confirming they always fall into two energy types.
description: Astronomers trained a neural network to search for fast radio bursts without complex data preprocessing. On the repeating source FRB 20121102A, it found 5,927 signals—three times more than before. Analysis showed that the burst energies always split clearly into two classes, and this property is unc
abstract: Fast radio bursts are enigmatic signals from space. Older search methods are like trying to listen to a radio drowning in static—faint flickers get lost. The new AI model, like a keen-eared listener, spots signals directly and found three times as many bursts from a single source. It turns out the energy clusters into two steady peaks—as if a lighthouse has two unwavering flashing modes.
threads: Обычные методы поиска космических радиовсплесков пропускали почти две трети сигналов. Новый ИИ-детектор смотрит на «цветную картинку» неба и видит невидимое: 5927 вспышек от одного источника вместо 2000. Это доказывает, что энергия всплесков всегда делится на два четких класса — как свет и тень от одной звезды.

title: Hydrogen Atmospheres on Scorching Planets: The Volcano Secret
oneliner: Rocky planets close to a star can hold onto a hydrogen envelope thanks to continuous eruptions driven by tidal forces.
description: Scientists have figured out how rocky planets hold onto lightweight hydrogen: stellar tidal forces heat up their interiors, triggering non-stop eruptions. Volcanoes belch out gases, replenishing the loss. Such a planet gives itself away by an unusually thin hydrogen envelope — which the James Webb S
abstract: Planets with hydrogen atmospheres usually lose them. But if a planet is on an elongated orbit close to its star, tidal forces heat it up and cause volcanoes that keep replenishing hydrogen. It's like a leaky bucket under a faucet: water escapes, but the faucet compensates. Scientists figured out which planets can hold onto such an atmosphere and suggested a way to find them. Will we find these worlds?
threads: Планета у самой звезды по вытянутой орбите. Приливные силы разминают её, как тесто, и заставляют недра извергать лаву. Водород, которому давно пора улететь, постоянно пополняется вулканическими газами. Тонкая водородная оболочка — признак кипящих недр. Телескоп Джеймса Уэбба сможет найти такие миры.

title: Invisible Object Weighing a Million Suns: Gravity Acts as a Magnifying Glass
oneliner: Astronomers have measured the mass of an invisible object weighing a million Suns for the first time by how it distorts light from a distant galaxy.
description: Using a network of radio telescopes, astronomers spotted a tiny flaw in a thin arc of a distant galaxy. It revealed an invisible object with a mass of about a million Suns — a hundred times lighter than anything previously weighed at such distances. The discovery proves that gravitational lensing ca
abstract: Astronomers spotted an invisible object in a distant galaxy: it warped light, like a tiny pebble on a still pond ripples a reflection. Its mass is just a million suns, a hundred times lighter than anything previously seen at such distances. This discovery proves that even the smallest clumps of dark matter can be detected. How deep can we peer into the hidden structure of the Universe?
threads: Астрономы нашли во Вселенной крошечный невидимый объект весом в миллион Солнц — по тому, как он исказил свет далёкой галактики. Эта «гравитационная лупа» позволила взвесить его с точностью до 3%. Так можно охотиться за тёмной материей и понять, из чего состоят маленькие галактики.

title: Listening to the Shudder of Space: The Nano-Mirror Experiment
oneliner: A tiny mirror, working like a tuning fork, will help physicists pick up the quantum shudder of space itself.
description: Physicists have proposed an experiment with a nano-mirror suspended on a laser beam. It vibrates like an ultra-sensitive tuning fork, and if space is foam-like at the micro-level, its oscillations will be disrupted. A special optical amplifier turns this disruption into an audible signal, opening a
abstract: Physicists suspect that at unimaginably small distances (the Planck length), space might behave oddly. To test this, they’ve set up an experiment with a tiny vibrating plate that catches the slightest deviations from familiar laws. Imagine listening to a faint echo from the depths of reality—what might it tell us about our world?
threads: Если прислушаться к тиканью сверхточных часов на нанозеркале, можно расслышать эхо от «пены» пространства. Экспериментаторы нашли способ усилить этот шёпот в миллиарды раз — и, возможно, впервые заглянуть за грань известной физики.

title: Photon Avalanche: How to Catch the Invisible
oneliner: A single microwave photon multiplies into dozens, making it detectable.
description: Ordinary detectors can't sense microwave photons—their energy is vanishingly small. A new superconducting device triggers an avalanche: one photon spawns dozens more, amplifying the signal and separating it from noise. This method paves the way for quantum networks and studying the echo of the Big B
abstract: Microwave photons are hard to catch one by one because of their tiny energy. Scientists came up with a device that turns one such photon into several, like a mountain echo amplifying a lone sound. This helps to detect them reliably. It makes you wonder: will we ever count photons as easily as we count raindrops?
threads: Один микроволновый фотон почти незаметен. Но с помощью сверхпроводниковой ловушки его можно заставить размножиться в десятки таких же частиц. Возникает лавина, которую легко засечь. Такой детектор способен считать каждый квант света, что откроет дорогу квантовым вычислительным сетям и исследованию реликтового излучения.

title: The Milky Way's Dark Halo is Flattened and Twisted
oneliner: The invisible envelope of dark matter around the Milky Way turns out to be flattened and twisted — like a deflated ball, rotated crosswise to the disk.
description: Using the motions of millions of stars, astronomers have built a 3D map of the dark halo. Its shape is not a sphere, but an elongated, flattened cloud. Surprisingly, the halo's long axis is almost perpendicular to the stellar disk. This orientation hints at a possible 'flip' of the disk in the past
abstract: Imagine dark matter around our galaxy as an invisible "scaffold" that you can "feel" through the movement of stars. It turns out this scaffold isn't round, but slightly flattened, and it's rotated differently than the visible disk: it's standing on edge! This hints that our galaxy flipped over in the past. What made the Milky Way do such a somersault?
threads: Тёмное гало Млечного Пути сжато с боков, а его длинная ось стоит вертикально звёздному диску. Это говорит о том, что наш диск когда-то перевернулся под действием гравитации мелких галактик-спутников. Невидимая «линза» объясняет таинственную плоскость карликовых галактик.

title: Quantum Record: 120 Entangled Qubits
oneliner: 120 qubits are woven into a Schrödinger's cat-like state for the first time—a record quantum ensemble.
description: A record entangled state of 120 qubits helps test quantum computers. Scientists used a clever scheme with temporary 'disentanglement' and error catching to bypass the fragility of connections. The achieved accuracy of 56% is an important step toward reliable computing and testing fundamental theorie
abstract: Picture this: 120 tiny particles acting like one perfectly coordinated choir. For the first time, scientists have managed to 'sing' a quantum song with such a large ensemble of qubits—it's like getting 120 pendulums to swing together perfectly. Each one helps check the quality of future quantum computers. Will we ever be able to 'tune' a thousand such particles?
threads: Физики спутали 120 кубитов в подобие «кота Шрёдингера» — рекордный квантовый ансамбль. Представьте оркестр из 120 музыкантов, играющих в унисон: малейшая фальшь разрушает гармонию. Учёные научились ловить ошибки, сохраняя хрупкую квантовую связь. Это шаг к надёжным квантовым компьютерам будущего.

title: A Scorching Planet Grew Two Tails
oneliner: The James Webb Telescope caught a scorching planet turning into a comet for the first time: its atmosphere boils off, creating two giant helium tails.
description: Astronomers using the James Webb Space Telescope observed the exoplanet WASP-121b—a blazing gas giant circling perilously close to its star. It turns out the planet is losing its atmosphere: helium streams into space in two comet-like tails. One tail outruns the planet and plunges into the star, whi
abstract: Astronomers have traced for the first time how the planet WASP-121b loses its atmosphere, like a comet, leaving behind two gas tails. One tail points toward the star, the other away from it. This discovery helps us understand how stars ‘blow away’ the atmospheres of nearby planets. What would happen to Earth if it were that close to the Sun?
threads: Оказывается, планеты могут быть похожи на кометы! Телескоп Уэбба разглядел у раскалённого гиганта WASP-121b два газовых хвоста: один падает на звезду, другой улетает прочь. Открытие меняет представления о том, как звёзды испаряют свои миры.

title: Comets around an alien star reveal their sizes
oneliner: The TESS telescope recorded 24 comet transits in front of the star RZ Psc, revealing their sizes: 1 to 7 km.
description: A swarm of comets around RZ Psc briefly dimmed the star's light as they passed in front of it. The TESS telescope caught these dimmings, allowing astronomers to estimate the sizes of these icy bodies—between one and seven kilometers. The size distribution resembled the belt of icy boulders beyond Ne
abstract: Scientists have found the second system in history where many comets pass in front of their star, like tiny eclipses. It turns out that the sizes of these icy bodies obey the same law as objects in the Kuiper Belt on the outskirts of our Solar System. Such 'tailed' swarms could be the key to unlocking the birth of planets. Maybe our Earth also began from a comet vortex?
threads: В 500 световых годах от нас телескоп TESS поймал 24 скоротечных «затмения»: это кометы проносились перед звездой RZ Psc. Их размеры подчиняются тому же небесному закону, что и ледяные глыбы пояса Койпера: много крохотных, мало крупных. Если запустить телескоп EVE, можно будет не только считать такие кометы сотнями, но и определять, из чего они слеплены.

title: Can the Moon have its own moon?
oneliner: Research shows: moons can have their own moons, and some of them could be habitable.
description: Astronomers investigated the possibility of submoons—small moons orbiting other moons. It turns out Earth could host an asteroid-sized submoon, while giant planets might have entire Earth-like worlds. But for life to emerge, a submoon needs about half of Earth's mass, and at that very mass its orbit
abstract: A submoon is a moon's moon. Tides determine its fate. Earth would only preserve asteroid crumbs, while warm Jupiters could host Earth-sized giants. But life requires a special mass: not too big and not too small. Such submoons are rare, like gold coins. Are habitable moons of moons a cosmic rarity?
threads: Луна может иметь свои собственные маленькие луны. Гравитация позволяет астероиду кружить вокруг Луны миллиарды лет. Но если такая луна-малышка будет слишком большой, она раскачает орбиту и улетит. Учёные говорят, что вокруг тёплых юпитеров такие луны могут стать настоящим домом для жизни. ✨

title: How Birds See Earth's Magnetic Field
oneliner: In birds' retinas, a protein called cryptochrome converts light and magnetic fields into an electrical signal, letting them see direction.
description: Scientists have figured out how birds navigate by the magnetic field. In their eyes, a protein called cryptochrome, under blue light, spawns a pair of field-sensitive particles. Their behavior shifts with the field's direction, and the protein generates an electrical signal. The bird's brain turns i
abstract: The study unpacks how birds feel Earth's magnetic field. In their eyes, a protein called cryptochrome, triggered by light, spawns particle pairs that respond to magnetism, firing off faint electrical signals—a bit like a compass needle swinging north. This keeps them on course. Could this kind of “biological vision” someday spark new navigation tech?
threads: Глаз птицы скрывает в себе компас – не иглу, а белок, превращающий магнитное поле в электричество. Открытие позволит создать крошечные датчики для навигации без GPS.

title: Dark Matter: An Invisible Web Explains Three Mysteries
oneliner: One dark matter model — three astronomical puzzles solved at once.
description: Simulations show that if dark matter could clump, its lumps would explain perturbations in the stellar stream GD-1, the mysterious object from the gravitational lens, and the origin of the Fornax 6 cluster.
abstract: Dark matter might be made of particles that interact with each other. Scientists have found that tiny clumps of such matter explain three puzzling phenomena at once: a bent star stream, a lens object, and a captured star cluster. It seems these invisible clumps act like cosmic magnets. Could one single idea solve several mysteries of the Universe?
threads: Невидимые «комочки» в космосе влияют на движение звёзд. Модель, созданная для объяснения странностей в звёздном потоке GD-1, неожиданно подошла и для других космических загадок. Это значит, что тёмная материя может цепляться сама за себя, слипаясь в плотные сгустки.

title: Rhythms of Black Holes: 34 Pulsations in Gamma-Ray Bursts
oneliner: Astronomers found 34 rhythmic oscillations in the emission of distant gamma-ray bursts, resembling the beat of a cosmic heart.
description: In data from the Swift telescope, 34 gamma-ray bursts with a clear rhythm were discovered. These bursts are born when a dying star collapses into a black hole, and its superheated gas swirls into a rapidly rotating disk. The rhythmic pulsations reveal the dance of matter around the hole: perhaps thi
threads: Гамма-всплески, рождающиеся при коллапсе массивных звезд, иногда «поют» с четким ритмом, словно космический метроном. Обнаружено 34 новых пульсации в излучении, и это намекает на танцы плазмы в дисках вокруг черных дыр, где само пространство-время закручивается вихрем.

title: Neural Network by Ear: How to Capture a Particle's Energy
oneliner: A neural network built into the detector listens in on the collider's noise and measures particle energy with record accuracy.
description: At the Large Hadron Collider, noise hinders precise particle energy measurement. A neural network embedded in the detector picks out the right signals and honestly assesses its own uncertainty — boosting the reliability of physics discoveries.
abstract: Scientists have developed neural networks that can precisely measure particle energies in the ATLAS detector, even against heavy background noise (like hearing a whisper in a crowded room). These programs fit onto compact boards and run in real time. In the future, they could help uncover new phenomena at the Large Hadron Collider. Will they be able to catch the signal that turns physics upside down?
threads: В коллайдере частиц какофония сигналов заглушает важные события, как крики толпы скрывают шёпот. Инженеры создали нейросеть-«суперслух», которая работает прямо в детекторе, точно измеряет энергию и честно признаётся, когда сомневается. Это приближает разгадку тёмной материи.

title: Quantum Simulator Discovers Plasma Memory
oneliner: In a quantum simulator, particles flew apart like an explosion but retained memory of the initial charge clumps.
description: Physicists simulated a collision of charged particles. Contrary to expectations, instead of chaos, a plasma emerged, expanding as a shock wave and retaining memory of the initial charge clumps. The reason — plasma oscillations akin to a pendulum. This discovery helps understand matter under extreme
abstract: Imagine a crowded dance floor where each pair starts dancing synchronously, creating waves that ripple across the room instead of immediately blending into the crowd. Physicists observed something similar: in a quantum experiment, particles and antiparticles formed a moving plasma that retained memory of the initial order. This discovery shows that even the simplest theories can surprise us.
threads: В квантовом симуляторе сгусток заряженных частиц не расплылся хаотично, а разлетелся прямыми потоками, помня о начальной форме. Такой баллистический плазменный сюрприз меняет понимание высокоэнергетических процессов и может пролить свет на тайны ранней Вселенной.

title: Tiny black holes from cosmic collisions
oneliner: Collisions of ultra-high-energy particles in space can give birth to tiny black holes, paving the way to understanding gravity at the atomic scale.
description: When particle rays collide in space, they can create microscopic black holes that instantly evaporate into a stream of neutrinos. Already, by studying neutrinos from a distant galaxy, scientists have tested energy limits where gravity changes the rules. Upcoming observatories will search even deeper
abstract: When cosmic rays collide, they might birth black holes and gravitons—if hidden dimensions exist. Neutrino data from a distant blazar already hints at the minimum scale of this ‘new physics’: M_f > 0.3 TeV. Future telescopes will push sensitivity to thousands of TeV. Imagine if black holes evaporate, leaving a one-of-a-kind neutrino trail?
threads: Две космические частицы, столкнувшись, могут родить чёрную дыру размером с атомное ядро. Она тут же испарится, вспыхнув нейтрино. Астрофизики уже ищут такие вспышки в данных нейтринных телескопов: если найдут — это укажет на скрытые измерения пространства.

title: Glowing Atoms Create a Nearly Perfect Engine
oneliner: When atoms shine in sync, a heat engine with near-perfect efficiency is born.
description: Synchronized radiation from atoms has led to a new type of heat engine: power grows explosively, and efficiency is near 100%. This paves the way for ultra-miniature devices with fantastic energy savings.
abstract: An engine based on atoms working in harmony, like a crew team: they simultaneously absorb and emit energy. This allows near-perfect efficiency from a single cold source. Imagine a battery that charges just by cooling down? This quantum effect could power the future.
threads: Группа атомов, вспыхивая синхронно, как стая светлячков, может создать двигатель с почти идеальной эффективностью. Ученые показали, что мощность такого квантового мотора растет квадратично с числом атомов, открывая путь к миниатюрным сверхэффективным устройствам.

title: Icy Wanderer Chiron Encircled by Rings and a Disk
oneliner: The tiny icy body Chiron sports a ring system—like its own miniature planetary system.
description: During an occultation of a star by Chiron, telescopes caught a series of blinks—three rings and a disk gave themselves away. The outermost ring lies in a zone where moons should be born, not rings. Comparison with old data shows the structure is changing, and we are witnessing the construction of a
abstract: The distant asteroid Chiron has been caught sporting three thin rings—like a miniature Saturn. They encircle this cold world like weightless hoops. But the most astonishing part: the rings seem to be forming right before our eyes! Will they turn into a new moon?
threads: Небесное тело Хирон, всего 200 км в поперечнике, обзавёлся кольцами. Астрономы, наблюдая за его прохождением перед звездой, заметили сложный узор из трёх колец, пылевого диска и далёкого объекта — возможно, так рождается новый спутник.

title: Supernovae Point to a Changing Dark Energy
oneliner: Accounting for the age of supernova progenitor stars reveals that dark energy is likely not constant, but changes over time.
description: It was previously thought that Type Ia supernovae were reliable 'standard candles' for measuring distances, their brightness independent of age. New research shows: old stars produce dimmer explosions. This distorts the cosmic ruler. Correcting for age removes the discrepancy, and the data leans tow
abstract: Type Ia supernovae are the key markers for measuring cosmic distances. But new research reveals: over time, these beacons ‘age’ and shine less brightly, like bulbs losing their glow. This shifts our picture of the universe’s expansion—perhaps it’s no longer speeding up, but slowing down. What if dark energy isn’t forever?
threads: Старые звёзды взрываются тусклее — это как если бы старые свечи давали меньше света. Астрономы обнаружили, что возраст прародителей сверхновых искажает измерение космических расстояний. После поправки оказалось: тёмная энергия, возможно, меняется, и ускорение Вселенной могло остановиться.

title: Two Probes Will Dive into the Tail of an Interstellar Comet
oneliner: In late October 2025, the Europa Clipper and Hera spacecraft will find themselves inside the tail of Comet 3I/ATLAS.
description: A comet from another star system will pass by two Earth spacecraft. In late October, they will spend several days in its tail—this is the first chance to directly study extrasolar material.
abstract: In late 2025, a comet from another star system will fly near two scientific probes. The probes might pass through its tail — like a boat cutting through a ship’s wake, but made of charged particles. This gives a unique chance to directly ‘touch’ material that came from far away. What will we learn about this cosmic wanderer?
threads: В конце октября две межпланетные станции 'нырнут' в хвост кометы, прилетевшей от другой звезды. Это первая возможность в истории 'пощупать' инозвёздное вещество прямо в космосе. Приборы зафиксируют, как невидимый шлейф частиц окутывает зонды, рассказывая о химии далёких миров.

title: Quantum Mind? Can't Do Without the Classical
oneliner: Quantum systems cannot think and act purposefully on their own.
description: Scientists have proved: purely quantum systems cannot become agents. To make plans and decisions, you need to copy information about the world, and the quantum no-cloning theorem puts an end to such attempts. The mind requires classical 'parts'. This explains how familiar behavior emerges in a quant
abstract: Can a quantum system act meaningfully? Research shows: purely quantum processes don't allow building models of the world and choosing actions — because of the no-cloning of unknown states and linearity. It's like trying to take a photo without a camera. So, at the core of agency there's always something classical.
threads: Квантовые частицы не могут копировать сами себя. Это не причуда, а математический запрет. Он не даёт чисто квантовой системе мыслить и принимать решения — для разума обязательно нужен кусочек классического мира. Возможно, наш мозг — смесь того и другого.

title: Noise That Helps: A Quantum 'Pendulum' on a Chip
oneliner: Errors on an IBM quantum chip helped create a stable time crystal — rhythmic oscillations that never fade.
description: Scientists simulated a magnetic material with a kagome pattern on a quantum computer. They found that noise from extra qubits doesn't disrupt but actually prolongs periodic oscillations (a time crystal), and sometimes even creates them. This is a way to control quantum states using unavoidable error
abstract: In the quantum world, orderly rhythms (time crystals) usually get wrecked by interference, but engineers found a way to harness noise for good. Picture a swing that gains momentum from random pushes. On an IBM processor, such noise helped build stable oscillations that last unusually long. Nature whispers: chaos holds hidden strength—where else might we uncover it?
threads: Шум, который обычно мешает квантовым системам, на чипе IBM сыграл роль дирижёра: он заставил «маятник» качаться дольше, создав подобие вечного тиканья. Этот трюк может сделать квантовые компьютеры стабильнее, превратив ошибки из врагов в союзников.

title: Quantum Bounce: How the Universe Became Uniform
oneliner: After a quantum bounce, the universe’s irregularities vanish on their own — explaining why the cosmos is so smooth.
description: Scientists have proposed a model where the universe didn’t explode from a point but first contracted and then bounced. Quantum effects during the bounce smooth out any distortions, making space uniform and the same in all directions. This is a natural explanation for the cosmos’s smoothness, requiri
abstract: The universe might have been born crumpled and uneven, but quantum effects, like an invisible baker's rolling pin, roll it into a smooth, flat pancake. In a new model, cosmologists showed that after a 'bounce' (instead of the Big Bang), any spatial distortion quickly disappears on its own, without help. The universe itself becomes homogeneous and isotropic. Maybe that's how smooth worlds are born?
threads: До Большого взрыва Вселенная могла сжиматься, а затем — отскочить. Ученые выяснили, что при таком отскоке все неровности сами собой разглаживаются, как складки на простыне при рывке. Вселенная становится идеально ровной без всякой настройки — загадка однородности решена.

title: Social Laser: Why We Act in Sync
oneliner: Disconnected people act as one—just like light in a laser.
description: Scientists applied the laser principle to crowd behavior. People are like atoms: they get charged by news and slogans. Once enough charge builds up, a single impulse triggers synchronized action. The model promises to predict mass events.
abstract: The social laser theory likens society to a laser. People, like atoms, absorb and spit out packets of social energy. When the heat hits a critical point, you get a synchronized burst—think revolution. It’s a lens for understanding why collective moods flip so suddenly and predictably.
threads: В лазере атомы вспыхивают разом, превращая разрозненный свет в направленный луч. Оказывается, общество иногда ведёт себя так же: когда достаточно людей «заряжено» одной идеей, сигнал из медиа может заставить всех действовать синхронно. Это объясняет, почему протесты или тренды вспыхивают внезапно, и намекает на возможность их предсказывать.

title: Coin-sized camera spots distant planets
oneliner: A 15 mm lens on the Hayabusa2 probe first spotted distant planets passing across their stars.
description: Scientists pointed the Japanese probe's navigation camera at alien suns and saw giant planets briefly dimming their light. Even such tiny optics, with clever data processing, can rival space telescopes. This trick promises to find planets that have been hiding for years.
abstract: Even with the smallest space camera, the size of a coin, we managed to 'catch' distant planets. It's like noticing the shadow of a fly against a streetlight several blocks away. Such observations help find worlds that big telescopes miss — what else might we glimpse through the eye of a needle?
threads: Камера размером с крупную монету на борту японского зонда «Хаябуса-2» впервые уверенно засекла проход далёких планет-гигантов по дискам своих звёзд. Этот успех означает, что крошечные инструменты смогут открывать неуловимые долгопериодические миры.

title: Stellar explosions puff up giant gamma-ray bubbles
oneliner: It’s not a black hole, but countless star explosions—like a giant cauldron boiling.
description: A computer model revealed that the gamma-ray bubbles at the Galaxy’s center are not the result of a black hole, but of vigorous stellar life. Like a boiling pot, massive star explosions hurl out particles that shine in gamma rays. The finding explained the Fermi telescope’s riddle and showed where t
abstract: The huge gamma-ray bubbles above and below the center of the Milky Way have long been thought to result from the activity of a supermassive black hole. New computer simulations show that similar structures can arise simply from the birth and death of massive stars. This discovery prompts us to rethink how galaxies breathe: perhaps our Galaxy is more 'alive' than we thought?
threads: Считалось, что гигантские гамма-пузыри в нашей Галактике надувает сверхмассивная чёрная дыра. Однако компьютерная симуляция воспроизвела их без неё. Оказалось, хватает обычных взрывов звёзд. Это открытие сулит новые способы изучать бурную жизнь галактик.

title: Resistance Paints the Secret Angles of a Magnetic Dance
oneliner: In thin films, a strange shift reveals the hidden behavior of magnetic arrows.
description: In bismuth ferrite coated with platinum, the resistance twists unpredictably—each microscopic magnetic arrow inside seems to point in its own direction. This glitch, never observed before, hints at the complex life of magnetic domains. It could lead to new memory based on magnetization vortices.
abstract: Scientists investigated how electrical resistance changes depending on the direction of a magnetic field in a special material. Usually, for antiferromagnets the signal shift is 90°, but here it turned out different from sample to sample — as if a compass pointed not north, but wherever it pleased. This unexpected discovery makes you wonder: what hidden magnetic forces are driving this process?
threads: Вы включаете ток в тонкой магнитной плёнке, а сопротивление рисует карту спинов. Обычно карта простая, но в новом материале она сбилась – словно компас показывает не на север. Такие аномалии сулят прорыв в спинтронике.

title: Tantalum Nucleus Reveals Electric Asymmetry
oneliner: Tantalum’s nucleus: a tiny skew that saved the Universe.
description: An electric dipole moment has been spotted in the tantalum nucleus — like a spinning top’s center of mass suddenly shifting. This imbalance, measured with record precision, sheds light on the mystery of missing antimatter and aids the hunt for dark matter.
abstract: Imagine the atomic nucleus not as a perfect sphere, but with a tiny electrical 'imbalance'. Scientists have measured such an imbalance—the electric dipole moment—for the nucleus of tantalum-181, using a superconducting current. This discovery could explain why there is more matter than antimatter in the Universe.
threads: Крошечная асимметрия в атомном ядре тантала указала на нарушение симметрии времени. Возможно, именно такие эффекты когда-то позволили материи победить антиматерию и создать нашу Вселенную. Новый метод измерения настолько чувствителен, что улавливает сдвиг заряда, сравнимый с шевелением пылинки на другом конце галактики.

title: The Grand Piano Chip: How Light and Sound Learned to Work Together Without Noise
oneliner: A rigidly fixed design dissipates heat 60 times better, allowing light and sound to interact cleanly.
description: Engineers unveiled a chip that’s not suspended but firmly anchored, dramatically improving heat dissipation. Heating distortions are reduced by a factor of 60, and quantum sound vibrations persist even under intense laser light. The technology opens the door to efficient microwave-to-light converter
abstract: In microchips, light and sound can work together, but they usually overheat. Scientists created a chip that stays cold even under bright light, like a freezer that doesn’t defrost from a lightbulb. This helps make quantum devices quieter and more powerful. Will we be able to hear the quantum whisper?
threads: Свет и звук плохо уживаются на микрочипах: малейший нагрев от лазера вызывает шум. Инженеры создали чип, который отводит тепло в 60 раз лучше, снижая искажения. Теперь можно подавать мощный свет без помех для квантовых звуковых волн. Это шаг к идеальным преобразователям микроволны-свет — основе квантового интернета.

title: A Quantum Instrument Plays the Music of Primes
oneliner: Physicists have created a quantum instrument whose notes exactly mirror the numbers that hold the secret of prime numbers.
description: Scientists constructed a quantum energy landscape whose allowed levels match the special points of the Riemann zeta function, which governs the distribution of primes. Corrections to the approximate model obeyed a simple rule that explains a mysterious fractal pattern and brings us closer to proving
abstract: They numerically constructed a quantum potential whose energy levels match the mysterious zeros of the zeta function (related to prime numbers). It's like tuning a violin to a special melody. It turned out that its fractal shape is explained by errors in the approximation. Could the quantum world hold the key to the secret of prime numbers?
threads: У чисел-призраков, скрывающих тайну простых чисел, нашёлся квантовый двойник. Учёные смоделировали систему, чья энергетическая гамма в точности повторяет нули дзета-функции. За регулярными поправками стоит простая математическая ошибка, и это может стать ключом к разгадке великой гипотезы.

title: Gravitational Waves Gain Overtones
oneliner: After merging, black holes ring in a chord: for the first time, a nonlinear overtone of gravitational waves is isolated.
description: Event GW250114 helped physicists capture not only the main wave from the black hole merger, but also the quiet overtones. These nonlinear oscillations, born from the self-interaction of spacetime, refined the black hole’s parameters and opened a new way to test relativity in strong fields.
abstract: After black holes merge, the new hole 'rings,' sending out gravitational waves — ripples in spacetime. In the signal GW250114, besides the main tone, scientists detected a faint nonlinear overtone — like an echo that shouldn’t appear in a simple model. This proves that gravity near a black hole is more complex and creates unexpected effects. What else is hidden in the cosmic ring?
threads: При слиянии двух чёрных дыр пространство-время звенит не одной чистой нотой, а с тихими обертонами. В событии GW250114 физики впервые чётко расслышали нелинейные гармоники — это открыло способ тестировать теорию Эйнштейна в самых суровых уголках Вселенной.

title: Earth's Shadow Hunts for Alien Probes
oneliner: Scientists have proposed a new way to search for extraterrestrial artifacts, using Earth's shadow as a filter.
description: Astronomers tested the idea of using Earth's shadow to detect alien probes. An automated telescope captured brief light flashes near the shadow's edge — objects not listed in any catalog. This could change the approach to the search for extraterrestrial intelligence.
abstract: Scientists have proposed hunting for alien objects near Earth using our planet's shadow. The shadow acts as a filter: inside it, the sky is darker, and dim artificial objects like probes become more visible. Early observations with the ZTF telescope have already uncovered unknown objects. What if alien emissaries are already here, but we just weren't looking in the right place?
threads: Астрономы впервые применили тень Земли как естественный фильтр для поиска инопланетных зондов. Обычно объекты вблизи Солнца теряются в его лучах, но тень позволяет их увидеть. В данных телескопа ZTF нашли несколько загадочных быстрых вспышек — возможно, это искусственные аппараты.

title: Quantum ions no longer need freezing cold
oneliner: Smooth laser tuning lets qubits operate accurately even with noticeable jitter, ditching complex cooling.
description: Ion-based quantum computers usually need near absolute zero to suppress particle jitter. A new “smooth gate” method sidesteps extreme cooling: changing the laser frequency during the operation itself cancels out excess jitters. It’s like gently stopping a swinging swing with a single precise push. E
abstract: Scientists have come up with a 'smooth gate' — a way to entangle ions in quantum processors. It's like gently slowing down a swing to keep it from swinging. The method allowed operations with very low error even without deep cold. Does this mean quantum computers will soon become warmer and faster?
threads: Крошечный ион в ловушке дрожит — и ошибается в квантовых расчётах. Но если плавно менять лазерный импульс, дрожь затихает сама, без ледяного охлаждения. Такой трюк открывает дверь к простым и надёжным квантовым компьютерам.

title: Magnetic Vortex Becomes a Quantum Bit
oneliner: In granular superconductors, magnetic vortices behave like two-state quantum systems.
description: In ordinary superconductors, magnetic vortices waste energy and cause interference. A film of aluminum grains traps a vortex in place, turning it into an ultrastable quantum bit. Scientists control it with microwaves, paving the way for new quantum computers and sensors.
abstract: In granular superconducting films, vortices turn into quantum switches—like tiny seesaws that you can nudge from one position to another. Scientists have learned to control them and measure their state without disturbing it. These 'living' vortices keep quantum properties for almost a millisecond—perhaps they'll become the workhorses of future quantum computers?
threads: Магнитные вихри в гранулированном алюминии ведут себя как квантовые биты — почти без потерь и с долгой памятью. Из них можно строить устойчивые квантовые компьютеры и датчики с атомной точностью.

title: Only Quantum Gravity Can Entangle Masses
oneliner: Regular gravity can't entangle two distant objects—quantum spacetime is required.
description: The study settles the debate: Einstein's classical gravity cannot create quantum entanglement between massive bodies. Only if spacetime itself acquires quantum properties does such a connection become possible. This is an important step toward a theory uniting gravity and the quantum world.
abstract: Two massive objects can be in a superposition—being in two places at once. Some thought that ordinary gravity, like an invisible thread, could connect them. But scientists refuted that: for entanglement to arise, the gravitational field itself must be quantum—not just a pull, but a wave. So, can gravity be both a force and a wave at the same time?
threads: Два массивных шара в космосе: толкните один, и второй не почувствует толчок мгновенно — гравитационные волны идут с запозданием. Но квантовая запутанность — это не волна, а нечто иное. Спор о том, способна ли обычная гравитация создать такую связь, разрешён: без квантового поля не обойтись.

title: How a Brainless Slime Mold Outpaces Supercomputers
oneliner: An ordinary amoeba, lacking a nervous system, can perform an astronomical number of computations — all thanks to the movement of its gelatinous mass.
description: Scientists have determined how many computational operations Physarum polycephalum — a slime mold devoid of neurons — can perform. By analyzing its growth and shape changes, they applied the physical limit of computation speed imposed by energy. It turns out that in a day, this blob of slime perform
abstract: Scientists have figured out how many computations the slime mold Physarum polycephalum can perform. This organism has no nervous system, yet it solves complex problems. Much like a plant reaches for light, it 'thinks' with its body, reshaping itself. In a day, it carries out up to 10³⁶ logical operations—more than the number of stars in the observable universe. Could this be the first glimmer of intelligence?
threads: Одна бесформенная капля слизи способна за день обработать больше информации, чем все компьютеры мира. Учёные выяснили, что амёба без мозга использует энергию на полную катушку, приближаясь к физическому пределу вычислений. Это открытие заставляет иначе взглянуть на природу мышления — может, разум вовсе не требует нейронов.

title: Quantum Communication Speeds Up Nearly Five Times
oneliner: Scientists have for the first time used a ten-light-channel trick to speed up entanglement between charged atoms.
description: Two charged atoms held in vacuum traps 1.2 km apart now become entangled almost five times faster by sending ten light pulses simultaneously — as if the post office dispatched ten delivery vans instead of one. This first-of-its-kind experiment with ion traps opens the door to a quantum internet.
abstract: Scientists have for the first time sped up the creation of a quantum link between two devices by sending light in batches—as if dispatching ten couriers instead of just one. This let them achieve a 4.6× speed boost and 96% accuracy over 1.2 km. Picture this: ten messengers dash off at once, and the connection is made as soon as the first one arrives.
threads: Квантовый интернет только что получил ускоритель. Физики заставили два иона «чувствовать» друг друга почти в пять раз быстрее, пропуская свет через десять временных каналов сразу — как многополосное шоссе для фотонов. Это первый такой трюк с ионными ловушками, и он обещает сделать квантовые сети намного быстрее, приближая эру защищённой связи и сверхмощных распределённых вычислений.

title: Superfluid Helium Feels How the Earth Spins
oneliner: A lab experiment will capture how Earth twists space-time.
description: Earth drags the fabric of space-time along with it — an effect predicted by Einstein. Scientists propose catching it with superfluid helium and a highly precise detector. The device will notice rotation slower than one revolution in the entire history of Earth.
abstract: An ultra-precise gyroscope based on quantum fluid helium can detect how the rotating Earth twists spacetime around it — an effect predicted by Einstein. It works like a spinning top, sensing the tiniest shudder from the planet "dragging" space. In just one second, it can notice a rotation a billion times slower than an hour hand. What would you feel if you could sense the rotation of the universe itself?
threads: Земля не просто крутится — она увлекает за собой ткань пространства, как ложка вязкий мед. Ученые разработали эксперимент со сверхтекучим гелием, чтобы уловить этот почти неуловимый эффект. Прибор настолько чувствителен, что может заметить поворот на один оборот за 4 миллиарда лет.

title: The Secret to Flawless Neon Ice for Quantum Computers
oneliner: Microwave resonators test neon films, laying the groundwork for error-free qubits.
description: Scientists grow smooth layers of frozen neon and check them with microwave sensors. A brief warm-up to 12 K makes the film uniform, allowing electrons to levitate over it as low-error qubits. This simple method paves the way for reliable quantum processors.
abstract: Electrons floating above frozen neon are candidate qubits. A smooth neon surface is critical. In a new study, microwave resonators—like road roughness sensors—checked the film's uniformity. A brief heat treatment made it noticeably smoother. Could this be the missing step toward stable quantum circuits?
threads: Твёрдый неон похож на идеально гладкий каток для электронов. Но малейшая рябь плёнки порождает ошибки в кубитах. Физики нашли способ выравнивать неоновый лёд, прогревая его до -261 °C, и проверять гладкость микроволновым «эхом». Это шаг к квантовым чипам, которые работают на левитирующих электронах.

title: Encounter with a Shutter: How a Photon Creates a Fountain of Probabilities
oneliner: A single photon, colliding with a shutter, gives rise not to a half, but to a fountain of probabilities.
description: When a single photon interrupts a fast shutter, it cannot be cut, and a mixture of states with different photon numbers emerges. Locally, it appears as a photon on the left and darkness on the right with a thin transition zone. This effect deepens our understanding of light and could improve precisi
abstract: A photon is a particle of light that can't be divided into pieces. But if you put a shutter in its path, you don't get half a photon—you get something strange: a mixture of different numbers of photons that on the left looks like a single photon, and on the right, like emptiness. It's as if light is 'smeared' between existence and nothingness. How is that possible?
threads: Одиночный фотон летит на камеру, но затвор захлопывается прямо перед ним. Разрезать его нельзя, и рождается квантовая смесь — бесконечный набор состояний с разным числом частиц. Слева свет, справа тьма, а между ними — парадоксальный переход. Такая игра со светом обещает новые приборы.

title: Dark Energy Is Not Constant: A New Look at the Universe's Acceleration
oneliner: Data from the DESI survey suggests that the mysterious force speeding up the cosmos may not be eternal or constant.
description: Analysis of galaxy maps, quasar light, and supernovae shows that the simple model of constant dark energy is losing to a version with a smoothly changing force. This needs verification, but it's intriguing.
abstract: Scientists used data from a new telescope to study dark energy—the mysterious force accelerating the expansion of the Universe. They found hints that dark energy might change over time, like a wind that strengthens and weakens, rather than staying constant. Is the fate of the Universe written in wavering shadows, or is it just a trick of the light?
threads: Космос расширяется, и что-то неведомое толкает его всё сильнее. Новые замеры с невиданной точностью показали: тёмная энергия не стоит на месте, она меняется. Если это подтвердится, наши учебники по астрономии придётся переписывать.

title: The Light Chip: A Treasure Map in a Sea of Chaos
oneliner: A new method turns the cryptic blueprints of light microchips into a clear map, highlighting the zones that make or break the device.
description: Engineers are leaning on computers more to design minuscule light circuits, but the output can be a whimsical mosaic. Now, scientists have a tool that shines a light on the weakest links, preventing expensive production flops.
abstract: Inverse design produces intricate optical microcircuits, but understanding their logic is nearly impossible. Scientists developed a method that highlights critically important areas — like a thermal camera revealing heat leaks. The slightest change in such an area amplifies signal loss by 11 times. So, where's the most vulnerable spot in your idea?
threads: Световые чипы, спроектированные компьютером, выглядят как хаотичная мозаика. Новый метод превращает их в карту сокровищ: подсвечивает пиксели, без которых всё перестаёт работать. Испытания доказали: повреждение «золотых» точек увеличивает потери сигнала в 11 раз. Так рождается язык, на котором нейросети объясняют свои загадочные решения.

title: Quantum Engines Powered by a Look
oneliner: Detecting a particle passing through a barrier can provide energy and even cool the system.
description: A quantum particle can seep through an energy barrier even if it lacks the strength to jump over it. If you place a detector right on the barrier, the very act of detecting the particle nudges it, taking away or adding energy. Thus, a microscopic engine emerges, running solely on observation—without
abstract: Quantum tunneling lets electrons slip through barriers. If you measure a particle inside such a barrier, it gains energy and can make a jump. This effect is the basis for quantum engines that produce power and cold just by observing. Imagine: a glance makes an electron leap over a wall. Could observation become fuel for nanomachines?
threads: Частица проходит сквозь стену, а когда её замечают — получает пинок энергии, становясь крошечной электростанцией. Такие «двигатели наблюдения» смогут питать наноустройства или холодильники для квантовых компьютеров без единой батарейки — достаточно просто смотреть.

title: Black Holes Where You'd Least Expect Them: Solving the Mass Gap Mystery
oneliner: Gravitational waves revealed black holes with masses of 50–70 Suns — right in the 'empty' zone of the mass gap.
description: It was thought that the most massive stars explode completely, leaving no black holes, so none exist in the 50 to 130 solar mass range. New observations found several such anomalies with low spin. The reason lies in the nuclear reaction rate inside stars, which shifts the boundaries of what's possib
abstract: Imagine giant stars exploding, leaving behind black holes. But some explosions are so powerful they leave nothing — hence the 'gap' in black hole masses. New data, though, show that massive, slowly spinning black holes have popped up in this gap. Why did nature change its mind?
threads: Взрывы сверхновых па́рной нестабильности должны оставлять «пробел» в массах черных дыр. Но новые данные гравитационных волн показали дыры прямо в этом пробеле — словно рыбы в пересохшей реке. Это меняет наши представления о жизни и смерти самых тяжелых звезд.

title: How to Braid Atoms to Save a Quantum Battery
oneliner: Giant atoms braided together suppress energy leaks, making quantum batteries long-lasting.
description: Quantum batteries quickly lose energy due to interactions with the environment. Researchers suggest braiding giant atoms together — the leak channels then cancel each other out, allowing useful energy transfer without losses. This is a step toward durable quantum storage devices.
abstract: In quantum batteries, environmental noise causes energy loss. To avoid this, giant atoms are arranged into a 'braid', where noise channels cancel each other out, like in noise-cancelling headphones. Could a 'braid' of atoms be the key to undying quantum devices?
threads: В квантовых батареях энергию крадёт шум, как невидимые трещинки в стакане. Физики придумали «косу» из гигантских атомов, где шум сам себя глушит. Такие батареи могут заряжаться без потерь и работать годами.

title: Whispering Chips: Quantum Sound for Hyper-Sensitive Gyroscopes
oneliner: Multiple taps on a chip force sound waves into a one-way whisper, slashing noise for ultra-sensitive rotation sensing.
description: Gyroscopes measure rotation but suffer from noise. A new chip design forces sound waves into a one-way flow, like whispers that travel only forward. By tapping the chip at multiple spots, backward-moving noise cancels out. The result is a gyroscope sensitive enough to detect the tiniest turns, usefu
abstract: Imagine sound waves on a surface, like ripples on water. These waves can measure rotation, just like the gyroscope in your phone. Scientists have found a way to use quantum effects to make them much more sensitive, by sending signals through multiple points — it's like listening from different spots to hear a whisper clearly. Could this lead to navigation systems that never lose their way?
threads: A gyroscope that hears rotation: on a tiny chip, sound waves are forced into a one-way lane, like a whisper traveling in only one direction around a dome. By tapping the wave at several points, noise nearly vanishes, revealing spins so faint they were once hidden. Soon, this quantum trick could steer drones and spacecraft with uncanny precision.

title: Tiny Seesaw Cooled Almost to Absolute Zero
oneliner: Physicists cooled a microscopic seesaw to 0.006 degrees above absolute zero—so cold that its swinging almost stopped, but not quite.
description: Using nuclear demagnetization—a method that extracts energy by removing a magnetic field—a 1.5-nanogram plate was passively cooled to 6.1 millikelvin. The residual jittering neatly obeyed the laws of thermodynamics. The record paves the way for gravitational wave detectors and testing quantum mechan
abstract: A tiny vibrating plate, weighing about as much as a couple of cells, was cooled nearly to absolute zero (down to just 0.006 degrees above) to calm its thermal jitters. Now it can pick up the faintest forces. What if, with its help, we could hear the motion of a single atom?
threads: Крошечную балку весом в полтора нанограмма удалось остудить до 0,006 градуса выше абсолютного нуля. При такой температуре её колебания стали едва заметны, но всё ещё подчиняются тепловым законам. Это открывает путь к датчикам, способным уловить гравитационную волну или толчок от одной тёмной материи.

title: The Hunt for Moons of Rogue Planets
oneliner: For the first time, the James Webb Space Telescope tested a method to find moons around lonely planets drifting without stars.
description: Astronomers analyzed data on the orphan planet WISE 0855 — a dim, cold ball not far away. For 11 hours, Webb watched its light, trying to spot the shadow of a moon. No direct discovery happened, but the method proved its worth. If many rogue planets have Titan-sized moons, we will soon find them.
abstract: Scientists used the JWST to hunt for moons around the solitary planet WISE 0855—like a wandering Earth without a star. Like a flashlight, the telescope caught shadows of passing moons, but found none. However, they calculated: if you check several such planets, the chance to spot a moon is high. How many lonely planets hide moons in the dark?
threads: У планет, летящих в темноте без звезды, могут быть свои луны. «Джеймс Уэбб» 11 часов всматривался в холодный шар WISE 0855 — не мелькнёт ли тень. Пока тихо, но доказано: если спутники есть, мы их скоро поймаем.

title: Electricity Reads Out Diamond Spins
oneliner: Scientists have found a way to read the quantum state of microscopic defects in diamond using electrical pulses — without complex optical setups.
description: The new method, CCDMR, replaces light with electrical signals to read quantum memory in diamond. It uses a process similar to developing photographic film: a laser writes spin information into electron traps, and then voltage and light release the charge, creating a measurable current. This opens th
abstract: Imagine: a quantum state is stored like a hidden image, then 'developed' with an electrical signal. This trick was pulled off with defects in diamond (quantum bits): instead of collecting rare flashes of light, spin information is transferred to long-lived charge traps. This paves the way for compact quantum devices—like digital film development.
threads: Алмаз может стать сердцем квантового компьютера. Ученые нашли способ читать его квантовую память не светом, а электричеством — как батарейку. Скрытые заряды часами хранят информацию о спине, чтобы выдать её по первому требованию. Это шаг к квантовым чипам размером с ноготь.

title: Quantum Chip Outperforms Supercomputers: Two Hours vs. Years
oneliner: An experiment on a quantum processor showed it solves a specialized task in hours, while classical computers would take years.
description: Scientists created quantum circuits that produce a specific result with high probability. The H2 quantum processor tackled the most complex one in two hours. The best classical simulation methods on supercomputers would take several years. This is a practical leap in quantum supremacy and opens new
abstract: Scientists ran a special program on a quantum computer that gives the right result in just a couple of hours—like instantly finding the one key that fits from a pile. Regular supercomputers would take years to do the same. This is another step toward quantum supremacy. What if this technology one day protects our data?
threads: Квантовый чип за два часа выдал результат, на повторение которого самым мощным суперкомпьютерам нужны годы. Это не магия, а квантовая интерференция: правильные ответы усиливаются, а неверные — гасят друг друга. Подобные схемы могут лечь в основу взломоустойчивого шифрования будущего.

title: A Black Hole Cooks Up Radiation Like a Pot of Soup
oneliner: A black hole’s hot radiation turns out to be a double copy of particles born cold in an electric field.
description: The double copy trick turns the cold birth of particles in a strong electric field into warm Hawking radiation. The horizon and temperature pop up by themselves, even though the original recipe doesn’t include them. This kitchen wizardry bridges gravity and the forces of the microworld.
abstract: Physicists have shown that Hawking radiation from black holes can be obtained from particle creation in an electromagnetic field via 'double copy'. It's like translating from the language of gravity to the language of particles: complex behavior becomes understandable. Could it be that the event horizon is just an effect of this translation?
threads: Физики нашли кулинарный секрет чёрных дыр. Метод «двойной копии» превращает простой рецепт супа (электрическое поле) в изысканное жаркое (гравитация). Оказалось, знаменитое излучение Хокинга — это всего лишь двойная копия рождения частиц в электричестве, где нет ни температуры, ни горизонта. Горячий эффект возникает из холодных ингредиентов. Такой трюк поможет склеить квантовый мир с тяготением.

title: Photo Editor for Quantum Computers
oneliner: The GANDALF algorithm cleans up noisy snapshots of atomic qubits, speeding up readout and reducing errors by a factor of 35.
description: Quantum computers based on neutral atoms are tripped up by slow state readout. The GANDALF program acts like a filter in a photo editor: it takes a short, noisy exposure and reconstructs a clear signal, doubles the speed of error correction, and slashes failures by tens of times.
abstract: Quantum computers based on neutral atoms compute like lightning but read results like molasses—picture a bullet train forced into a long station stop. Scientists found a way to speed up readout by cleaning noisy signals, a bit like sharpening a blurry photo. This slashes errors and makes quantum error correction dramatically faster. Imagine getting an almost instant answer instead of waiting for minutes.
threads: Каждый кубит светится всего несколькими сотнями фотонов. Алгоритм, вдохновлённый фоторедакторами, угадывает состояние по зашумлённым снимкам. Квантовые компьютеры перестают ждать и начинают считать — с ошибками в 35 раз реже.

title: Star explosions accelerate particles to record energies
oneliner: New observations prove that supernova remnants can accelerate protons to energies hundreds of times higher than those of Earth's accelerators.
description: The LHAASO observatory studied gamma-ray emission from supernova remnant IC 443 and found that particles there are accelerated to record energies with no signs of slowing down. This is direct confirmation: it's star explosions that produce the most energetic cosmic rays bombarding Earth.
abstract: Shock waves from exploded stars, like giant pistons, accelerate particles to incredible speeds. New observations of supernova remnant IC 443 have shown: protons reach energies of hundreds of teraelectronvolts—billions of times more than the most powerful Earth-based accelerators. Could this be how cosmic rays that permeate our entire Galaxy are born?
threads: В китайском созвездии Тельца есть туманность — след от взрыва звезды. Оказывается, этот «звёздный пепел» работает как гигантский ускоритель, разгоняя протоны почти до скорости света. Энергия одной такой частицы в миллионы раз выше, чем в Большом адронном коллайдере. Теперь астрономы видят, что именно так рождаются космические лучи, непрерывно бомбардирующие Землю.

title: Quantum Predator-Prey Games
oneliner: Physicists had atoms act out the predator-prey drama on a quantum lattice.
description: The Lotka-Volterra model, familiar from the population fluctuations of foxes and hares, now works in the quantum world. Scientists lined up giant atoms and made them simulate population battles. Quantum effects don't disrupt but sustain these cycles — a path to the ultrafast simulators of the future
abstract: Scientists have created a quantum analog of the predator-prey model (like foxes and rabbits) on an array of Rydberg atoms. On microsecond timescales, atomic excitations play the roles of predators and prey, exhibiting stable cycles. This opens the door to simulating ecosystems in quantum labs. Could other ecological rhythms emerge in atomic "jungles"?
threads: Зайцы и лисы — классика. Но что если в роли хищников и жертв выступят возбуждённые атомы? На квантовой решётке такие «гонки» идут за микросекунды и не сбиваются помехами. Это не просто игра — так инженеры учатся управлять неравновесными квантовыми системами для компьютеров будущего.

title: Quantum Leap: A Day Instead of 22 Years
oneliner: Calculations for turning carbon dioxide into fuel accelerated from 22 years to a single day.
description: Researchers sped up a quantum computer by a factor of 8000, making the conversion of CO₂ into fuel a reality. For the first time, such a machine outperformed a conventional one on a practical task.
abstract: Quantum computers could turn carbon dioxide into clean fuel, but previously the calculations would have taken decades. A new approach combined improvements at every level of the quantum system and cut the time from 22 years to one day — like swapping a horse and buggy for a jet plane. Is large-scale green fuel from CO2 now within reach?
threads: 22 года — именно столько понадобилось бы квантовому компьютеру, чтобы найти способ превратить углекислый газ в топливо. Но инженеры нашли «секретный соус», ускорив расчёты до одного дня. Теперь зелёная энергия из воздуха — не фантастика, а реальный план на ближайшее будущее.

title: Diamond spies peek at magnetic secrets under Earth's core pressure
oneliner: Spy defects in diamond have captured superconductivity in titanium at 180 gigapascals for the first time.
description: Scientists have learned to create spy defects in diamond: swapping a carbon atom for nitrogen yields a sensor that responds to magnetic fields. For the first time, they've observed titanium under nearly two million atmospheres expel magnetic fields—a sure sign of superconductivity. This technology p
abstract: Imagine a diamond with tiny defects that act as magnetic field sensors. Scientists have improved them so they withstand pressures millions of times greater than atmospheric.
